title: Jurassic Park
description: One of the most beloved modern pinball machines. A choose-your-own-adventure through Isla Nublar — rescue workers, capture dinosaurs, and try not to get eaten. Deep rules but a satisfying core loop even if you only scratch the surface.

summary.text: Jurassic Park is a choose-your-own-adventure through Isla Nublar. The core loop is a five-step cycle called the Paddock Loop: (1) hit the three M-A-P Shots (white-glowing shots scattered across the playfield — watch the letter inserts for the active one) to open a paddock, (2) shoot the Paddock Entrance (the brightly lit center shot, glows the paddock's color) to rescue workers, (3) hit the Set Trap Standup Targets (small cluster of standups just left of the truck ramp — they go yellow after 3 rescues) to arm your trap, (4) shoot the Helipad Ramp (right ramp, right flipper) to call the chopper, (5) shoot the Paddock Entrance again to capture the dinosaur. Each captured dinosaur earns a big score chunk and moves you to the next paddock. Alongside the main loop, bashing the Truck (the large toy dead-center of the playfield) advances T-R-E-X letters toward a T-Rex event, and alternating between shots and the truck spells C-H-A-O-S for a massive multiball. There's a lot going on — but if you stay focused on the Paddock Loop, you'll have a great game without touching any of the rest.

one_shot.name: The Helipad Ramp
one_shot.description: The right ramp. It's Step 4 of the Paddock Loop — calling the helicopter before you can capture the dinosaur. You can't finish a paddock without it. Feed it from the right flipper, hold through the curve. Missing it stalls your entire loop and costs you the capture. Make it your most-practiced shot on this machine.

key_areas.name: The Truck
key_areas.location_hint: Dead center of the playfield, unavoidable.
key_areas.why_it_matters: The truck controls which paddock opens when you spell M-A-P, and it advances T-R-E-X letters every time you hit it. It also deflects balls unpredictably. You will interact with it constantly — sometimes on purpose, mostly not. Knowing it's there and roughly where it sends balls is the baseline for not being surprised on this table.

key_areas.name: The M-A-P Shot Zone
key_areas.location_hint: Three rotating white-lit shots scattered upper-left to upper-right across the playfield.
key_areas.why_it_matters: These are how every Paddock Loop starts. They don't live in one fixed place — the active letter moves after each hit, so you're always scanning. Until reading which one is glowing becomes automatic, the whole loop feels random. This zone is the engine.

key_areas.name: The Paddock Entrance
key_areas.location_hint: Center playfield, lit in the current paddock's color when active.
key_areas.why_it_matters: The shot you return to more than any other in the game. Every rescue attempt, every capture — all five steps of the loop eventually come back here. Burning this location into muscle memory is the single fastest way to improve your loop time.

key_areas.name: The Set Trap Standup Cluster
key_areas.location_hint: Left-center, just left of the truck ramp.
key_areas.why_it_matters: The most-overlooked step in the loop. Players rescue three workers and then start shooting randomly because they don't know what changed. When these standups go yellow, you're stalled until you hit one. Know where this cluster lives and everything else clicks into place.

key_areas.name: The Helipad Ramp
key_areas.location_hint: Right side of the playfield, fed from the right flipper.
key_areas.why_it_matters: Step 4 of 5 in the Paddock Loop. You can't capture a dinosaur without it. This is also your One Shot That Matters — it earns its place here too because missing it when you're this close in the loop is the most common place players stall mid-game.

hard_box.title: What Makes This Game Hard
hard_box.text: The rules are deep and the Paddock Loop has five steps — it's easy to lose track of where you are, especially when the truck keeps getting in the way of shots you're trying to make. The biggest mistake average players make is chasing T-R-E-X letters or Chaos Multiball instead of staying on the Paddock Loop. Both are fun. Neither pays like capturing dinosaurs. The other killer: the playfield is busy enough that you'll accidentally advance things you didn't mean to. That's fine — but it can pull your attention in five directions at once. Stay on the loop.

priorities.title: Open Your First Paddock
priorities.description: The three M-A-P Shots are the white-glowing shots scattered across the playfield. Watch the inserts — the letter inserts light white when a shot is active. Hit each one to spell M-A-P. The truck direction determines which paddock opens: bash the Truck Target to cycle between left and right paths on the map. You don't need to optimize your paddock choice on Ball 1 — just open something and get in.

priorities.title: Work the Paddock Loop
priorities.description: Once the paddock is open, shoot the Paddock Entrance (the lit center shot — it's hard to miss when it's glowing) to rescue workers. The screen shows your worker count — you need 3 rescues before traps become available. When the Set Trap Standup Targets (the cluster of small yellow-lit targets just left of the truck ramp) light up yellow, hit one to arm the trap. Then shoot the Helipad Ramp (right ramp, earnable from the right flipper) to call the helicopter. When the chopper is called, shoot the paddock entrance one more time to capture the dinosaur and bank your score.

priorities.title: Let T-R-E-X Happen Naturally
priorities.description: Hitting the Truck Target during your normal play advances the T-R-E-X letters on the display. You don't need to hunt these — you'll hit the truck accidentally on your way to other shots. If T-Rex Multiball starts, great. If not, don't worry about it. Your energy belongs on the Paddock Loop.

priorities.title: Pick Up Where Ball 1 Left Off
priorities.description: Progress carries over between balls. Check the display when you plunge — if you're mid-paddock, you don't need to re-spell M-A-P. If you are at the start of a new paddock, hit the white-glowing M-A-P Shots to open it. Ball 2 is about consistency — one complete Paddock Loop (rescue, trap, helipad, capture) is a great ball.

priorities.title: Try the Smart Missile Once
priorities.description: The Smart Missile Ramp is the left ramp. It grabs the ball and pauses it at the top, giving you one shot to thread it into the Amber Mine (the captive ball target behind the truck, upper-center playfield). The awards are substantial — spotted rescues, add-a-balls, bonus points. It's a hard shot under pressure. Try it once on Ball 2. If you miss, move on. Kineticist calls mastering this shot the bridge between good and great scores — save that lesson for when the paddock loop feels automatic.

priorities.title: Start a Control Room Mode if You See It Lit
priorities.description: The single C Target (top-center, just above the truck) starts Control Room modes when lit. If you notice it glowing, a shot or two there adds a mode on top of whatever you're already doing. Don't chase it — but if it's lit and you're at the top of a ball, it's worth the detour.

priorities.title: Complete as Many Paddock Loops as Possible
priorities.description: Ball 3 is all-in. Captured dinosaurs are your score. Stay laser-focused on the five-step Paddock Loop: M-A-P Shots → Paddock Entrance (3 rescues) → Set Trap Standup Targets → Helipad Ramp → Paddock Entrance (capture). Each complete loop is worth significantly more than casual shot values. Two complete loops on Ball 3 is a strong finish.

priorities.title: If Chaos Multiball Is Close, Consider Going For It
priorities.description: Chaos Multiball requires spelling C-H-A-O-S by alternating between a shot and the Truck Target. Each letter alternates: shot → truck → shot → truck → shot. If you're at C-H-A-O and on Ball 3, consider finishing it. With multiple balls live, you'll accumulate points fast. But if Chaos is only at C or C-H, the Paddock Loop is still more reliable.

priorities.title: Use Your Ball Saves and Move Fast
priorities.description: On Ball 3 with nothing to lose, play a little faster. Don't panic — but don't over-coach yourself either. The ball save from a plunge gives you a reset; the ball save from side drains buys you a retry. Take your shots with confidence. If it drains, it drains. You played the loop.

items.title: The Raptor Pen Is Your Idle Shot
items.description: When you have no clear priority shot, shoot the Raptor Pen Ramp (left orbit, feeds smoothly back to the right flipper). It's a low-risk backhand from the right flipper and advances Raptor Pen progress as a side benefit. Better than letting a ball roll to the center and draining while you decide what to do.

items.title: Know Where the Drain Dangers Are
items.description: Jurassic Park has a wide-open Center Drain and outlanes that punish loose balls. The truck sits in the middle of the playfield and redirects balls unpredictably. When in doubt, trap on a flipper rather than panic-flipping. A trapped ball is a ball you control.

items.title: Watch the Truck Direction Before You Plunge
items.description: The Truck Target determines which paddock opens when you spell M-A-P. Check the display after plunging — it shows the current map path. If you want to switch directions, hit the truck once (easy from either flipper, just let it roll down-center after a plunge). Changing it mid-ball costs you a shot but saves you from opening a paddock you didn't want.

items.title: Trap and Read Between Shots
items.description: This playfield is busy. The display updates constantly and inserts change state after every hit. After a big shot, catch the ball on a flipper and take two seconds to read what lit up. Knowing whether you need M, A, or P next — or whether the Set Trap Targets just lit — saves you from shooting randomly.

items.title: Don't Chase T-R-E-X Letters
items.description: Deliberately hunting the Truck Target to spell T-R-E-X pulls you off the Paddock Loop and often results in a center drain. You'll hit it plenty by accident. T-Rex Multiball is fun, but it doesn't score like captured dinosaurs do.

items.title: Don't Start Chaos Multiball on Ball 1 or 2
items.description: Spelling C-H-A-O-S requires an alternating sequence of shots and Truck Target hits. It's a long, aggressive commitment and it pulls you completely off the Paddock Loop. Save any Chaos attempt for late Ball 3 when you've already had a solid loop or two. For most league players most nights, Chaos isn't worth planning around.

items.title: Don't Stress About the Map Path
items.description: The paddock map has branches and edge routes that advanced players optimize. Don't worry about it. Any captured dinosaur is progress. Pick a direction and commit. Agonizing over which paddock to chase is how you end up draining while reading the display.

items.title: Don't Force the Smart Missile on Ball 3
items.description: The Smart Missile Ramp threading is a great shot when you're comfortable — but on Ball 3 with the game on the line, a missed smart missile attempt often results in a bad bounce off the truck and a drain. If it's not feeling natural tonight, skip it. Stick to the loop.

between_us.label: The Rules Are Deep. The Core Loop Isn't.
between_us.text: Jurassic Park has one of the most complex rulesets in modern pinball. Pro players talk about map edge optimization, spinner value loading, and Chaos stacking. Ignore all of it for now. The five-step Paddock Loop is the entire game for league play. Two complete loops in a ball is a great ball. Three is exceptional.

between_us.label: Your Score Is Mostly Captured Dinosaurs
between_us.text: It's tempting to try to play everything — Control Room modes, T-Rex Multiball, Chaos, the smart missile — because they're all exciting. But your score on this game is almost entirely a product of how many Paddock Loops you complete. A player who captures four dinosaurs in a ball will outscore a player who starts two multiballs and misses the captures every time.

between_us.label: The Truck Is Not Your Enemy (Mostly)
between_us.text: New players curse the truck for deflecting shots. It does. But the truck is also the game's biggest free point dispenser — hitting it advances T-R-E-X, helps spell C-H-A-O-S, and usually feeds somewhere useful. Think of it as a pinball bumper that occasionally works in your favor. Aiming around it is a skill that comes with time on the machine.

between_us.label: Progress Survives Between Balls
between_us.text: Unlike some games, Jurassic Park remembers where you were in the Paddock Loop between balls. If you had two workers rescued when you drained, you start Ball 2 needing one more rescue before traps light. Check the display after plunging every ball — it tells you exactly what step you're on.

playfield_callouts.name: M Shot
playfield_callouts.description: One of three rotating white-lit shots that spell M-A-P. Location varies based on which are active. Watch for the white glow on playfield inserts — when M is active, the lit white shot on the left side of the playfield needs to be hit. Feed from the left flipper.

playfield_callouts.name: A Shot
playfield_callouts.description: One of three rotating white-lit shots that spell M-A-P. When A is active, the lit white shot in the center-upper area of the playfield needs to be hit. Varies with game state — always watch the inserts, not a fixed location.

playfield_callouts.name: P Shot
playfield_callouts.description: One of three rotating white-lit shots that spell M-A-P. When P is active, look for the white-lit shot on the right side. Feed from the right flipper. Completing M-A-P opens the current paddock.

playfield_callouts.name: Truck Target
playfield_callouts.description: The large physical truck toy sitting in the center of the playfield. Hit it to advance T-R-E-X letters and change the truck's direction on the map (which determines which paddock opens after spelling M-A-P). Easy to hit from either flipper — balls often hit it accidentally in the normal course of play.

playfield_callouts.name: Paddock Entrance
playfield_callouts.description: The lit center shot that opens when a paddock is active. It glows brightly (color matches the current dinosaur's paddock) and is usually a direct center or slight-left shot from either flipper. Shoot it for each rescue attempt, then again after the trap is set and the helicopter is called.

playfield_callouts.name: Set Trap Standup Targets
playfield_callouts.description: A small cluster of standup targets just left of the truck ramp, lower-center playfield. They light up yellow after you've completed 3 worker rescues in the current paddock. Hit any one of the yellow-lit targets to arm the trap. They're an earnable backhand from the right flipper.

playfield_callouts.name: Helipad Ramp
playfield_callouts.description: The right ramp — the main right-side shot. Feed from the right flipper with a firm hold through the curve. This is Step 4 of the Paddock Loop (calling the helicopter). Must be completed before you can capture the dinosaur. Also accessible as a backhand from the left flipper, though that's a harder shot.

playfield_callouts.name: Raptor Pen Ramp
playfield_callouts.description: The left orbit shot — sends the ball around the top-left of the playfield and feeds back to the right flipper. A clean, low-risk idle shot when you're between priorities. Feed it as a backhand from the right flipper or a forehand from the left. Advances the Raptor Pen paddock as a secondary benefit.

playfield_callouts.name: Smart Missile Ramp
playfield_callouts.description: The left ramp — captures the ball at the top and gives you a pause to aim the captive ball shot at the Amber Mine. Harder than the Helipad Ramp. Feed from the left flipper. The ball pause gives you a moment to aim the follow-up shot, but the angle on the Amber Mine is tight.

playfield_callouts.name: Amber Mine
playfield_callouts.description: The captive ball target in the upper-center of the playfield, behind the truck. Only reachable via the Smart Missile Ramp pause. Hit the captive ball to collect a Smart Missile award (spotted rescues, add-a-balls, bonus points). A hard shot — the angle is tight and the truck blocks your view.

playfield_callouts.name: C Target
playfield_callouts.description: A single standup target at the top-center of the playfield, just above the truck. When lit, hitting it starts a Control Room mode (Keypad, Shutdown System, or Restore Power in sequence). Easy to hit accidentally during normal play. When you see it lit, a deliberate shot there adds a mode layer on top of your current play.

playfield_callouts.name: Center Drain
playfield_callouts.description: The gap between the flippers. Jurassic Park's playfield is open enough that the truck frequently deflects balls toward the center. Watch for the ball rolling down the middle after a truck hit — this is where most unexpected drains happen. Trap the ball on a flipper when in doubt.

sources.title: Flip N Out Pinball — Jurassic Park Tutorial (Joel)
sources.description: Joel's walkthrough of the full Paddock Loop, Control Room modes, and Chaos Multiball spelling. Primary source for the ball plan structure and loop step descriptions.

sources.title: Kineticist — Jurassic Park Strategy Guide
sources.description: Detailed tactical breakdown including Smart Missile technique, edge path strategy, and shooter lane tips. Source for Level Up content and Smart Missile attribution.

sources.title: System-J Pinball — Jurassic Park Tactics
sources.description: Deep rules breakdown with specific advice on spinner value optimization and T-Rex Multiball stacking strategy. Referenced for Level Up advanced tips.

sources.title: Pinside Owners Club — Jurassic Park (Stern 2019)
sources.description: Community source for 2X scoring awareness and common player questions about the paddock loop steps.

sources.title: Stern Pinball — Jurassic Park Feature Matrix
sources.description: Manufacturer spec sheet with Pro/Premium/LE feature comparison and mech descriptions.

chat_starters: What's the Paddock Loop and how do I start one?
How do I know when to hit the Set Trap targets?
Should I go for Chaos Multiball at league?
What does the truck direction actually do?

level_up.intro: Jurassic Park has one of the deepest rulesets in modern pinball. Once the Paddock Loop feels automatic and you're completing two or three per ball, these strategies will push your scores significantly higher.

tips.title: Stack T-Rex Multiball Into a Paddock Mode
tips.description: T-Rex Multiball doesn't score much on its own — but it's a free 2-ball multiball you can bring into an active paddock. Spell T-R-E-X by hitting the Truck Target, wait for the multiball to start, then intentionally drain to single ball with your paddock loop progress intact. Now complete the paddock with a safety net ball in play. This is a core intermediate strategy that System-J details specifically.

tips.title: Master the Smart Missile Threading
tips.description: Kineticist identifies the Smart Missile as the shot that separates good from great players on JP. After the left ramp grabs the ball, you have a pause window to aim at the Amber Mine captive ball. The target is tight — aim slightly left of center. The awards (spotted rescues, add-a-balls, major bonuses) are large enough that consistent Smart Missile use dramatically increases your score. After 5+ rescues across the game, Extra Ball can appear as a Smart Missile award.

tips.title: Layer Control Room Modes Into Paddock Captures
tips.description: Starting a Control Room mode (via the C Target) while you're mid-paddock creates a layered scoring situation. If you have a Control Room mode ready and are about to start a capture sequence, hit the C Target to start the mode, then immediately shoot the Paddock Entrance. Both score simultaneously during the capture window. More active modes = more points per shot.

tips.title: 2X Scoring — Recognize It When It Starts
tips.description: The Supply Drop target (far right standup) lights 2X scoring for everything on the playfield. This often starts somewhat serendipitously during multiball when balls bounce into the Supply Drop area. When you see 2X lit, your next few shots — especially captures and ramp completions — are worth double. Recognize it and take your best shots in that window.

tips.title: Take the Edge Path to Visitor Center
tips.description: The paddock map has a center path and edge paths. Kineticist specifically recommends staying on the edges for competition play — the center paddocks (Spitter, T-Rex) are harder to complete and the Visitor Center is the mini-wizard mode payoff. Plan your truck direction before each paddock to route around the center. Left-left or right-right paths tend to be faster routes to the wizard mode.

tips.title: Spinner Value Loading
tips.description: System-J details a specific setup: the spinner value increases based on tower, helipad, and both ramps being shot since the last spin. If all four are lit and you have Super Spinner available (from the right inlane), the spinner can deliver serious points per rip. This is situational and not worth chasing in a casual league game, but in a tight tournament situation with the right setup, a fully loaded spinner shot can matter.
